title: AAIF Conformance Self-Certification Report
description: A machine-readable, self-published statement of a platform's AAIF (SC-006) conformance. Published at /.well-known/aaif-conformance.json. Lets orchestrators, CI pipelines, and the adopter registry verify a platform's claimed conformance level and supported registries before dispatching agents. See SPECIFICATION §Y and CONFORMANCE.md. Self-certification is honour-based but machine-checkable: the claim references a public test-suite result.

generated_at.description: When this report was produced (ISO 8601 UTC).

expires_at.description: Optional expiry; consumers SHOULD re-fetch after this.

aaif_versions.description: AAIF schema versions this platform supports.

report_url.description: Canonical URL of this report (typically the .well-known path).

manifest_url.description: URL of the platform's AAIF Platform Manifest (§U).

claims.description: One claim per (level, direction). A platform may be e.g. Producer:Enterprise and Consumer:Portable.

direction.description: 'producer': exports conformant AAIF; 'consumer': imports and honours it; 'both'.

corpus.description: Identifier/commit of the conformance corpus run (e.g. 'SC-006@3.4.0 tests/conformance').

log_url.description: Optional link to a public CI run.

roundtrip.description: Optional interop evidence: frameworks this platform losslessly round-trips for the level's field set.

lossless_fields.description: AAIF field paths preserved through framework→AAIF→framework.

supported.description: Registry coverage (ids from registries/*.json the platform implements). Lets orchestrators match agents to platforms.

attested_by.description: Name/role of the person or team certifying.

contact.description: Email or URL for conformance questions.

signature.description: Optional base64 Ed25519 signature over the canonical JSON (excluding this field).
